observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the MA to consistently and promptly prepare BRSs to ensure the fair presentation of the CIB-LCCA and LCTD accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE (a) establish clear, standardized policies and procedures for asset donations and assign a department or office to oversee the process; and (b) direct the MA to (i) derecognize from the books the Construction and Heavy Equipment and Building that were inappropriately recorded; and (ii) review the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) prepared by the MAO staff to prevent similar accounting errors and ensure the accurate reporting of the PPE account balances.

We recommended that the LCE (a) request the SB to expedite the review of the Municipality’s LRC for updating in accordance with RA No. 7160; and (b) direct the MT to (i) seek the assistance of barangays in inspecting and verifying the status of the 1,737 businesses for appropriate action; (ii) examine taxpayers' books of accounts or use available records in her office to validate the declared gross receipts, particularly for businesses engaged in transactions with the Municipality; (iii) verify, assess, and validate other businesses other than the noted business taxpayers and take appropriate action to recover the under-collected business taxes amounting to ₱385,362.39; and (iv) enforce stricter implementation of business tax regulations in accordance with the LRC to ensure accurate and uniform tax collection.

We recommended that the LCE, as head of the MDC, (a) require the other members of the MDC to (i) refrain from appropriating the DF for non-eligible expenditures; (ii) ensure that programmed PPAs are implementation ready and specifically provided in the budget and AIP; and (iii) maximize the use of the DF by fully appropriating the fund for development PPAs of the Municipality.

We further recommended that the LCE instruct the ME to prepare the ABC for the infrastructure projects in accordance with the pertinent provisions of the DPWH DO No. 197.

We recommended that the LCE require the HRMO Head, the MBO, and the MA to: (a) continue their strict adherence to existing rules and regulations, as well as the Municipality’s control measures in the engagement, management, and payment of salaries for Casual, JO, and COS personnel; and (b) conduct periodic assessments of the internal controls to ensure their effectiveness and adaptability to any changes.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the MDRRMO Head, in coordination with the MDRRMC, to (i) ensure that only eligible, disaster-related expenditures are included in the LDRRMFIP; (ii) implement only those projects included in the approved LDRRMFIP; (b) refrain from granting cash advances to the MDRRMO Head, who was not an authorized SDO for LDRRMF- funded projects; (c) require the MT to ensure that disbursements are made directly to the suppliers through check; and (d) require the BAC to strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 on documentation,  posting, and reporting requirements on procured projects to substantiate the regularity of the Municipality’s procurement process.

We recommended that the LCE require the GFPS to (a) continuously implement, monitor, and assess the efficiency of fund utilization to achieve the desired outcomes for gender equality initiatives; and (b) promptly notify the DILG Office in writing of any changes made to its endorsed GPB base on the annual budget for consistency and accurate reporting.

We recommended that the LCE: (a) direct the MA and HRMO Chief to (i) regularly reconcile deduction records to ensure accurate remittances and alignment with billing statements from GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth; (ii) strengthen internal controls for manual processing, ensuring a review and validation process before finalizing entries in the payroll; and (iii) promptly adjust any discrepancies of the affected accounts to reflect accurate balances; and (b) consider adding an additional employee to the HRMO to address the current staffing shortage and improve the efficiency of their operations.

We also recommend that the LCE direct the concerned officials to continuously comply with the regulations on the withholding of taxes from income payments, premiums/contributions, and loan amortizations from the salaries and wages of Municipal officials and employees, as well as the timely remittance of withheld amounts and employer's share to the respective government agencies.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to initiate and coordinate a thorough reconciliation with the Department of Health (DOH) to verify the accurate balances of ‘Due to NGA-DOH’ account. Following the reconciliation, the Municipal Accountant shall make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances and ensure consistency between the records of the municipality and the DOH;
Require the Municipal Accountant to record expenditures paid through Trust Fund in their appropriate expense accounts and close the same to appropriate account at year-end. Debit only ‘Due to NGAs’ account upon submission of liquidation to source agency; and
Conduct a detailed review of the dormant fund transfers to identify the reasons for inactivity and assess whether the funds are still relevant or necessary for their intended purpose. Send confirmation letter to all possible source agency in order to verify the balances recorded and adjust the funds received from PhilHealth and the Red Orchid award to ‘Trust Liability’ account.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the balances of Unexpended LDRRMF as shown in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002 for fair presentation and to enhance the understandability of the users of Financial Statements.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to return to the Source Agencies the unexpended/unutilized balances in the Due to NGA account and henceforth, ensure that unspent funds should be returned to the source agency after completion of the project.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Municipal Engineer to submit the Project Logbook, Weather Chart, Geotagged Pictures or any supporting documents the work suspension for further evaluation by the Audit Team;
Direct the Municipal Engineer to require the contractor to submit documentary evidence supporting the claimed delay in customs clearance at the port of entry and that the delay of issuance by the government agency was not primarily caused by him; and
Henceforth, ensure that any similar requests must be supported with proper documentation and carefully evaluated to be within the bounds of law before they are granted by the Municipality

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Instruct the Municipal Development Council to update the Annual Investment Plan through a revisit of the unimplemented development projects and review its viability and necessity, taking into considerations and strictly observing the guidelines set in DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020, as well as other budgeting guideline;
Ensure that all projects funded by Supplemental Budget were monitored and implemented on time; and
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat to accomplished properly the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) that includes all projects to be procured and implemented for the year as basis for all its procurement transaction as required under Section 7.2 of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to strictly observe Section 5 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013, and Item 4.3, NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 14, 2024.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to cease the practice of charging prior year’s expenses against the current year appropriations. Spend within the budget and ensure to strengthen budgetary controls that no obligations are incurred without available appropriations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive as co-chairperson of the Local School Board:

Ensure that approval of the budget is covered by a LSB Resolution and the receipt of the budget by the Local Budget Officer, the Local Accountant, and the Local Treasurer is properly documented for efficiency and transparency purposes.;
Ensure that the Local School Board submit quarterly and annually the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 to the DepEd CO through appropriate channels, copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG not later than the 20th day after the end of the quarter and for annual reports, not later than the 15th day of February of the following year; and
Instruct the LSB Secretary to post the utilization report of the SEF Budget in at least three (3) conspicuous public places and in the Full Disclosure Policy Portal of the DILG not later than the 20th day after the end of each quarter and to record the date of posting as documentation of compliance with the Joint Circular.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Require that GFPS Chairperson be a signatory of Box A of the Obligation Request for expenditures charged from the GAD Budget; and
Prohibit charging of expenses that are not identified in the GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) Chairperson to submit the Gender and Development (GAD) Plan and Budget within five working days from its approval.

In light of the foregoing, we recommended that Local Chief Executive:

Establish and strengthen the gender-responsive monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system by issuing the necessary Executive or Administrative Order and formally constituting the GAD M&E Team;
Through the monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system, conduct regular assessment of institutional mechanisms for gender mainstreaming to monitor the effectiveness and efficiency of GAD implementation; and
Prepare and ensure the timely submission of the annual LGU Status Report on Institutional Mechanisms for Gender Mainstreaming to the DILG Regional Office.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the above-mentioned SB officials to refund immediately the overpayment of RATA totaling ₱87,475.00. Henceforth, give only a proportionate amount of RATA based on the actual services rendered.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the above officials and employees to refund the excess claim of travel expenses in the total amount of ₱15,840.00 and henceforth, ensure that future claims of travel expenses adhere strictly with Executive Order no. 77.

documentation of CF to strictly submit/attach the required documents to the cash advance
voucher prior to payment; and (b) MA to (i) submit the cash advance voucher for CF and
its attachments within seven days after the release of check; and (ii) recognize the

the details of each PPE account and prepare an adjusting entry to correct the PPE
account balances; and (iii) strictly adhere to the proper classification of accounts
according to RCALGU under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

the SLs in the present Accounting System of the MAO; and (b) require the MA to (i)
exert vigorous efforts to work back on previous years’ records and coordinate with
the PGB and MTO to reconcile their records; and (ii) draw a Journal Entry Voucher
to correct the erroneous recording of collections/income to the Due to LGUs account.

We therefore recommended that management undertake the following remedial measures:
Enjoin the Office of the Accounting and Treasury to reconcile their records regularly to avoid accumulation of variance; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to draw journal vouchers for valid reconciling items.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to send a written request to the Source Agencies (SAs) for a copy of the MOA for the subject fund transfers; and
Initiate the preparation of MOA between the NGAs, if such were not constituted on the subject fund transfers tied with specific purposes and ensure that future fund transfers from NGAs are supported with MOA and other necessary documents.

Revisit the MEEDO Code and if the executive departments find the relevant provisions on market lease do not serve well the interest of the local government, the lessees, and the general public, make amendments to provisions of the Municipal Ordinance No. 029-2020 to be presented to the Sangguniang Bayan.

6.6 We recommended that the LCE, as head of the MDC (a) continue to improve
and enhance the Municipality’s development planning and project prioritization by
ensuring that the manner of appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF is
invariably compliant with Section 3.2.5 of DILG, DOF, DBM JMC No. 01 dated
November 4, 2020; and (b) include in the AIP only PPAs that would materially
contribute to the development of the Municipality for the benefit of its constituents.

7.23 We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MDRRMO Officer and
LDRRMC to (i) properly plan and prepare the LDRRMFIP for the current and
prior year’s balance of the fund with details of the PPAs for proper monitoring and
reporting of the accomplishments of the Municipality; and (ii) recommend and
prepare a revised LDRRMFIP in case of changes in the PPAs and new expenditure
items and require the approval by the SB; (b) Municipal Budget Office (MBO)
Officer and the MA to charge only eligible items against the Fund pursuant to the
regulation; (c) MDRRMO Officer to submit a report on the distribution made to
account for the issuance of IEC materials; (d) BAC to submit reasonable
justification on the disparity of price and excessive procurement of IEC materials;
and (e) MA to comply with the proper accounting treatment for AP based on the
actual receipt of goods.

8.16 We recommended that the LCE (a) require the GFPS and Focal Person to (i)
implement the PPAs as planned; (ii) coordinate with the DILG Office in the event
of changes in the implementation of GAD PPAs and submit the revised GPB thereto
for approval; and (iii) set measurable targets and disclose quantifiable actual results
in accomplishing the GPB and GAD AR; and (b) issue an EO for the (i) creation of
M&E Team to strengthen the M&E System for gender-responsive PPAs; and (ii)
reconstitute the GFPS pursuant to existing regulation.

9.21 We recommended that the LCE through the MENRO (a) embark on
effective projects and programs that support the Municipality’s ecological solid
waste management program through re-use, recycling, and composting activities for
an effective solid waste diversion as embodied in its SWM Plan; (b) coordinate with
the Municipal Agriculture Office and barangays to formulate a distribution plan of
fertilizers from biodegradable wastes for agricultural use to prevent the piling
thereof at the MRF; and (c) facilitate the immediate repair of equipment and
request a higher voltage from Meralco at the MRF to make use of the rapid
composter for purposes of increasing the processing of wastes to fertilizer.

10.7 We recommended that LCE instruct the MT to (a) immediately require the
Lexsys Technologies Incorporated to address and fix the inaccuracies found in the
CCS and its Subsystems; (b) require the AF Custodian to manually prepare the
CRAAF to report an accurate balance of unused AFs in the stockroom and in the
custody of the AOs; and (c) submit the CRAAF on or before the fifth day of the
ensuing month.

11.5 We recommended that the LCE require the MA and MT to continue to
comply with BIR regulations on the withholding and remittance of taxes within the
prescribed period.

12.6 We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA and the MT to continue
to comply with the withholding of contributions/premiums and loan repayments
and remittance thereof to the GSIS including the Municipal share within the
prescribed period; and (b) require the MA to recognize the Due to GSIS account for
loan repayments of Municipal officials and employees concerned for appropriate
reporting in the books.

13.6 We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA and the MT to
continuously comply with the correct and prompt withholding of
contributions/premiums and loan repayments and remittance thereof to the GSIS
including the Municipal share within the prescribed period; and (b) require the MA
to recognize under the Due to Pag-IBIG account instead of the Due to GOCCs
account the loan repayments of Municipal officials and employees concerned for the
fair presentation of the account in the books.

14.4 We recommended that the LCE require the MT and MA to continue to
comply with regulations on the withholding of PhilHealth contributions and
remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive, when the budget permits, to consider the installation of an Information Technology Office tasked to set-up, administer and maintain the official website of the Municipality to effectively promote transparency and accountability of the procurement processes and activities of the agency.

We recommended that the  LCE require the (a) MGSO to (i) conduct periodic inventory of supplies; (ii) prepare the corresponding RPCI and submit the same to the MAO for reconciliation with the reported inventory balances in the books; (iii) maintain  stock cards for the receipt and issuance of   supplies and materials; and  (iv) prepare and submit to the MAO the monthly SSMI for issued inventories; (b) MA to maintain supplies ledger cards for all inventory accounts to support the recorded balances of inventory; and (c) MGSO and MA to perform regular  reconciliation of the balances from the records maintained by their respective Offices to arrive at  reliable balances of the  inventory account.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Inventory Committee  to conduct an annual physical inventory of PPE and submit the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year to avail of the “One-Time” Cleansing of PPE account balances, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006; (b) MGSO  to (i) report all obsolete and unserviceable PPE in the IIRUP duly signed by the Head of Accounting Unit, Inspector/Appraisal Committee and approved by the Local Chief Executive  or authorized representative, and submit the report to the Disposal Committee for proper disposition; (ii) keep the unserviceable PPE in a secure place or storage room and dispose  them within a reasonable time; and (iii) maintain and update property cards per category of PPE and Property Acknowledgement Receipt for issuance to end-users; and (c) MA to  (i) derecognize unserviceable properties from the books upon disposal thereof based on the  approved IIRUP; (ii) update the PPELCs to reflect the correct balance of each account and (iii) provide the MGSO with the list of all insurable assets and insure all these properties  with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS pursuant to  COA Circular Nos. 81-160  dated April 21, 1981  and 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA to (a) conduct a thorough review and analysis of all trust payables and determine the reasons for their non-liquidation;  (b) exert effort to identify the source agencies; send confirmations thereto to be able to obtain the correct balance of the amounts to be settled and to facilitate the liquidation of all fund transfers; and (c) prepare complete liquidation reports; settle the  accounts;  and return the unutilized/unspent balance to the source agencies.

We recommended that the LCE continue to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development fund and require the: (a) Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO), Municipal Engineer (ME), and the Implementing Offices to continuously monitor the status of project implementation to ensure the prompt and timely execution of targeted PPAs; and (b) MA to submit the lacking documentary requirements to reliably support the deficient claims.

We recommended that the LCE (a) continuously ensure the implementation of all PPAs and maximize the utilization of available funds to address the delivery of services required to mitigate and alleviate the recovery of the Municipality from the adverse effects of the COVID-19 pandemic; and (b) require the MA to (i) recognize in the books under Trust Fund the transfer of the unexpended balances of DRRMF as  Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account; and (ii) submit the lacking documentary requirements to reliably support the claims thereof.

We recommended that the LCE, being the Chairman of the GFPS, (a) instruct the GFPS through the MPDO to establish a GAD database for gender statistics and sex disaggregated data to serve as inputs for planning, budgeting, programming and policy formulation; (b) strive for the full implementation of GAD-related PPAs by utilizing judiciously the budget set aside for said purposes; and (c) comply with the provisions of the aforementioned Circular on the submission of GAD AR to facilitate audit of the utilization of GAD budget and the efficiency and effectiveness of interventions in addressing gender issues.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA and the Municipal Treasurer (MT) to continue to be compliant with the BIR regulations on the withholding of the correct amount of taxes due and the remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to verify the prior year’s transactions affecting the cash in bank accounts and to recognize in the agency books the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the  Local Chief Executive require the: (a) Supply Officer/GSO to (i) prepare the stock cards for the purchased supplies; (ii) conduct periodic inventory of supplies; (iii) prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Inventories and submit the same to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with the reported inventory balances in the books; (iv) prepare and submit to the Accounting Office the RSMI for issued inventories and henceforth, prepare  the RSMI  on a monthly basis; and (v) support issuance of inventories with properly approved Requisition Issue Slips; (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) record the purchases under the proper Inventory account as Asset instead of as Expenses; and (ii) maintain supplies ledger cards for all inventory accounts to support the recorded balances of inventory.

We also recommended that the (c) Municipal Administrator, when the budget permits, to (i) consider the provision of a storage room for the procurement of supplies in significant quantities; and (ii) assign a storekeeper to effectively discharge property accountability and maintenance of records and reports.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the: (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare the necessary adjustments to reclassify the asset items classifiable as  Machinery and Equipment, Furniture and Fixtures, Books and Other Property, Plant and Equipment and related accumulated depreciation to the appropriate accounts and thereafter, to observe the ₱15,000.00 capitalization threshold when classifying/recording  these tangible items as PPE; (ii) derecognize unserviceable properties from the books upon disposal thereof based on the  approved Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property (IIRUP); (iii) assess the economic value of the PPE as basis for the recognition of impairment loss; (iv) provide the General Service Officer with the list of all insurable assets and insure all these properties   with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS pursuant to  COA Circular Nos. 81-160  dated April 21, 1981  and 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.

We also recommended that the  (b) General Service Officer  (i)  conduct the annual physical inventory and submit the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; (ii) maintain complete and updated property cards per category of PPE and updated Property Acknowledgement Receipt for issuance to end-users; (iii) report all obsolete and unserviceable PPE in the IIRUP duly signed by the Head of Accounting Unit, Inspector/Appraisal Committee and approved by the Local Chief Executive  or authorized representative, and submit the report to the Disposal Committee for proper disposition; (iv)keep the unserviceable PPE in a secure place or “bodega” and dispose  them within a reasonable time; and (v) strictly observe the guidelines and procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020   in the Conduct of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE), Recognition of PPE Items Found at Station, and Disposition for Non-existing/Missing PPE Items, for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the: (a) Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer to (i) continue to monitor the status of project implementation to ensure the prompt and timely execution of PPAs under the 20% Development fund; (ii) integrate to the overall project cost under Pay Item “SPL-Construction Safety and Health” a lump sum amount to be quantified in the detailed estimate to ensure the occupational safety and health condition of workers and the general public in the project site/ location; (iii) properly plan the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) of the 20% Development Fund and refrain from charging expenditures that do not partake the nature of capital investments or those not essential or relevant to the attainment of desirable socio economic development and environmental management outcomes; (b) BAC secretariat to submit the duly approved CSHP to preclude the issuance of a notice of suspension in the post-audit of the transactions; and (c) Municipal Accountant to require the approved Construction Safety and Health Program (CSHP) prior to the processing of the contractor’s claim either for advance payment or first progress billing.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balances of DRRMF other than those appropriated for capital outlays to the Trust Fund and recognize the balance as Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account.

We recommended that  the Local Chief Executive  instruct the (a) implementing offices of the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities  to (i) comply with the posting and  the reportorial requirements on  the monthly report on fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format; (ii) post the accumulated reports on the LGU’s website and in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the locality at the end of every month; and (iii) and  send a written notice to the regulatory and oversight agencies when said reports have been posted on its website; and (b)  Bids and Awards Committee secretariat to post the Notice of Award,  Contract and Purchase Order in the website of the Procuring Entity (PE) or if none, any conspicuous place in the premises of the Procuring Entity and the GPPB online portal for Emergency Procurement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) remind the created Municipal Project Monitoring Committee Team  to give due priority on  the monitoring of the implementation of GAD PPAs, evaluate and report the level and extent that GAD PPAs have become effective and efficient in checking GAD issues; and (b) instruct the GAD Focal Point System Technical Working Group and secretariat to strive for the full implementation of GAD-related PPAs by utilizing the whole amount set aside for said purposes so that the desired outcomes are attained and benefits are enjoyed to the fullest extent by  the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with the BIR regulations on the withholding of the correct amount of taxes due and the remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with the regulations on the withholding and remittance of monthly contributions due to the GSIS for the employees’ immediate enjoyment of their benefits.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to comply with regulations on the withholding and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with regulations on withholding and remittance of monthly PhilHealth contributions within the prescribed period.
We recommended that LCE direct the GSO to exert efforts in the preparation or maintenance of PCs for control and monitoring to prevent undetected loss of PPE.

observations.recommendation:
Post the Notice of Delinquency in the Payment of Real Property Tax and other taxes in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay of the municipal government.

Avail of the civil remedies provided for in Chapter IV, Title I, Book II of R.A. 7160 and develop other feasible strategies to enhance collections of delinquent taxes from taxpayers.

Conduct periodic reconciliation of Real Property Tax Receivable with the Accountant and Assessor and to establish database for each individual Real Property Taxpayers.
observations.managementAction:
The office of the Municipal Treasurer as mandated by law is directed to exert effort to maximize collection of local taxes, fees & charges. It is discouraging that even we have done our best and initiated collection remedies but still there remain uncollected real property taxes amounting to P3,978,404.98 because of the following reasons,to wit:

1. Duplication of some Tax Declarations

2. Boundary Conflict – between LGU Calamba and LGU Lopez Jaena

3. Big communication companies such as Smart & Globe failed to pay their taxes. 

4. Our office have only one (1) notice server, Covering 33 barangays

5. Difficulty was encountered in tracing the actual owner because of transfer of ownership
On the other hand, the uncollected business taxes of P545,786.26, it includes regulatory fees & penalties.

	The following are the reasons, to wit:

1. There were many business establishment had closed their operations during the year  and transfer to another place.

2. There were businessmen who pay their business taxes & fees in the barangay only, even if there gross receipts above P30,000.00

3. Our office were not able to conduct actual inspection,Because there is now a Joint Inspection Team created for inspection purposes.

This year the Municipal Treasurer hereby commits to comply and implement the recommendations as stated in the AOM.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

Due to pandemic, collection efficiency of delinquent taxes is low.

observations.recommendation:
1.15 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the concerned
Municipal officials to (a) provide an allowance for impairment on receivable accounts
based on analysis of collection rating in the previous years to ensure the fairness of
presentation of the Receivable accounts in the financial statements; (b) retrieve the
documents supporting the excess remittance of taxes withheld and make a
representation to the BIR for possible refund or tax credit; and (c) carefully monitor
the taxes withheld and remitted to the BIR to prevent the same deficiency in the
future.

2.13 We recommended that the LCE (a) as head of the Local School Board instruct
its members to ensure that only expenditures prescribed by existing rules and
regulations are charged from the SEF; (b) require the Municipal Accountant to (i)
ensure that expenditures are supported with complete documentation prior to
payment; and (ii) stop the grant of cash advance to school officials for DepEd
activities instead allow the grant of cash advances to designated disbursing officers of
the Municipality of Plaridel; (c) require the district supervisor, in coordination with
the Municipal Engineer, to follow the standards/specifications set by DepEd and
DPWH in the execution of the projects on the repair and maintenance of school
facilities; and (d) require the DepEd officials to submit the necessary liquidation
documents for the cash advances totaling ₱446,415.00.

3.7 We recommended that LCE direct the GSO to exert efforts in the preparation
or maintenance of PCs for control and monitoring to prevent undetected loss of PPE.

4.12 We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Engineer to (i)
submit the above-listed detailed engineering documents to the Audit Team for further
technical review and evaluation of the projects; (ii) ensure that detailed engineering
documents are submitted to the Audit Team prior to the implementation of the
projects; and (iii) require the contractors concerned to repair the defective and
damaged portion of the road projects pursuant to the one year warranty from the date
of project completion up to final acceptance or the defects liability period; and
(b) Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documentary
requirements for infrastructure projects prior to payment.

5.11 We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the (a) MDRRMO
to prepare and submit to the AT the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization
of LDRMMF within the prescribed period; (b) concerned officials to refrain from
using the LDRRMF for transactions which are not disaster-related; and (c)
Municipal Accountant to ensure that expenditures are supported with complete
documentation prior to payment.

6.9 We recommended that the LCE (a) require the Municipal Engineer to comply
with the standard design specifications of projects to be funded from LGSF-AM
Program to ensure optimum use of the local road facility and road safety; and (b)
monitor the implementation of the projects to ensure the timely completion and
delivery to the intended beneficiaries.

7.5 We recommended that the LCE submit a report of detailed expenses charged
from the GAD with supporting documents to validate the propriety and efficiency of
the disbursements.

8.11 We recommended that the LCE strictly monitor and intensify the information
campaign among the barangay residents of the no segregation, no collection policy of
the MOP.

9.4 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer to continue to be complaint with the BIR regulations on the
withholding of the correct amount of taxes due and the remittance thereof within the
prescribed period.

10.5 We recommended that the LCE continue to be compliant with the regulations
on withholding and remittance of monthly contributions due to GSIS for the
employees’ immediate enjoyment of their benefits

11.4 We recommended that the LCE remain to be compliant with the regulations
on withholding and remittance of monthly contributions due to PagIBIG for the
employees’ immediate enjoyment of their benefits

12.5 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to make
the necessary adjustment amounting to ₱394.50 and the HRM Officer to immediately
notify the Accounting Office of any modification in the payroll to adjust recording in
the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile with the Municipal Treasurer the balances of the specific accounts to
establish the correct CIB balance per bank account in the TF books as well as the
corresponding related asset accounts and make the necessary adjustments after
reconciliation to reflect the correct balances of the respective accounts in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
prioritize the review of the RPTARs to provide the Municipal Accountant of a
complete Certified List of Taxpayers with amounts due and collectible so that the
Municipal Accountant will be able to make the proper RPT/SET receivable setup at
the beginning of the year and provide the breakdown/schedule of RPT/SET
receivable balances at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to
submit to the Municipal Accountant a monitoring report stating the condition and
the fair value less cost to sell of the biological assets as of year-end of CY 2019.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all cash advances are liquidated at the end
of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to update the cashbook for the affected cash accounts and
reconcile with the ledger balances of the Municipal Accountant; and
b. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to henceforth reconcile the
balances of their respective records, at least quarterly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to
continue in exerting extra efforts to complete the titling process of the
aforementioned parcels of land.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require all departments to submit their respective PPMPs with complete details,
manner, and schedule of procurement to the BAC prior to its inclusion to the APP to minimize reimbursement;
b. consider other suppliers of petroleum products in nearby municipalities who
are willing to supply their products following the guidelines set forth in RA No.
9184; and
c. require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all
applicable taxes are withheld and remitted within the prescribed period in
accordance with tax laws and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit
the RPT shares of the barangays on a quarterly basis to provide for the immediate
use of the funds in various projects/programs of the Barangays.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to remit
the unexpended balances to its respective Source Agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
submit to the Audit Team the CA voucher and its supporting documents for all CAs
for CF within 7 days after the release of check and submit the LR to ICFAU prior to
the recording of its liquidation in the books of accounts to comply with the
requirements of the Joint Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. require all Municipal Collectors to prepare the RCDs and remit collections to the
MT on a daily basis; and
b. indicate in the RCDs the date such collection was received from the Municipal
Collectors and ensure that collections are deposited within the prescribed
period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the designated General
Services Officer to:
a. exert effort in locating all the necessary documents for the registration of the
two vehicles and immediately facilitate its registration; and
b. closely monitor its annual registration to avoid penalties and charges.

We have recommended that management:
Require the PCH Head to timely submit a Schedule of Receivables at year-end to the Office of the Municipal Accountant to serve as basis of the latter to record the transaction in the books of accounts of the hospital;
Instruct the Chief of Hospital to make representations to Philhealth to inquire the reason for certain receivables/ cases that have remained long outstanding and for hospital officials to immediately comply thereon; and
Enter into Memorandum of Agreement with DSWD- Misamis Occidental to document/improve existing mechanism and help strengthen the collectability of PCH’s promissory notes.

We have recommended that management:
Require the pharmacist to submit monthly record of inventories and issuances and forward the same to the municipal accountant;
Instruct the pharmacist and the municipal accountant to reconcile stock card records with that of the supplies ledger cards; and
Direct the municipal accountant to effect adjustments in the inventory and expense accounts and stop the practice of recording inventoriable items as outright expense.

We have recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to reconcile the inventory report and accounting records. Likewise, we recommend that management enjoin department heads to fully cooperate if there are unreconciled PPE items traceable from their respective departments.

We have recommended that management direct the LDRRMO to include and implement in the LDRRM Investment Plan the deficiencies noted relative to compliance on the minimum standards for disaster preparedness. Likewise, we enjoin collaborative discussion thereon with the Sangguniang Bayan on the implementation and augmentation of the LDRRM Fund, if need be.
We have recommended that management direct concerned PCH and Municipal Officials to strictly comply with the provisions of SB Ordinance 6, s. 2017.

We recommended that Management (a) secure a copy and enforce strictly the provisions stipulated in the loan agreement entered into by the delinquent borrowers with the MSWD and exhaust all means to recover from defaulting borrowers; and (b) if the circumstances so warrant, initiate the preparation of the request for authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off receivables which remained outstanding in the books to be supported with the documents as itemized in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management coordinate with the BIR for the filing of claim for tax credit or refund, if still acceptable, or if not, initiate the preparation of the request for authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off receivables which remained outstanding in the books to be supported with the documents as itemized in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated under Observation No. 1 of the 2019 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  (a) instruct the General Services Officers to improve the process of inventory management by keeping track of the movement of stocks from receipt until issuance for accounting,  control and accountability purposes; initiate the preparation of stock cards for the purchased supplies; and conduct periodic inventory of supplies as prescribed by  regulations; (b) consider the provision of budget for a storage room so that the procurement of supplies in significant quantities can be undertaken to lessen the administrative cost in frequent procurement; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant to improve compliance on the recording of purchases under the proper inventory account and to maintain supplies ledger cards for delivered stocks.

We recommended that Management direct the GSO to (a) exert efforts to secure the transfer certificate of  title for the  lot purchased by the Municipality; (b) request the COA Auditor to determine  the  appraisal cost  of the  unserviceable property and effect the immediate disposal and  derecognition  from the  Accounting books  and  Property Records; and (c) maintain Property Cards and Real Property Cards for land for easy reconciliation of the account;

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Local Development Council (a) continue to invest in high-impact development projects in the utilization of its 20% Development Fund so that the best possible desired level of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes may be achieved/attained; and (b) ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion period through close supervision and monitoring to maximize the development benefits and the early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management continue to be compliant with the criteria on disaster readiness to prevent loss of lives and properties arising from natural disasters or calamities and aim to further equip its MDRRMC personnel with advanced training on disaster preparedness and rescue operations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive continue to be compliant with the rules and regulations in the utilization of the financial assistance under the LGSF/ADM Program to assure the attainment of its objective to alleviate the living condition of the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that Management (a) continue to exert best efforts to be fully compliant with the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 by strictly administering the segregation of wastes at source to minimize the cost being incurred in the waste disposal by the contractor and the Municipality’s garbage trucks; and (b) involve closely the remaining seven (7) barangays to adopt  the  strict  implementation  of  the Anti-Littering campaign of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management (a) continue to comply with the GAD program on the formulation and submission of GAD plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report; and (b) establish and submit the details of accomplishment reports together with its supporting documents to validate the expenses charged to GAD fund.

We recommended that Management continue to be compliant with Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 on the withholding of the appropriate taxes from its payment of transactions and ensure remittance thereof within the prescribed period and continue to remit all other collections held in trust due to Other National Government Agencies and Government Owned Controlled Corporations within the prescribed period of ten (10) days after the end of each month.

We reiterated our recommendation of that the Municipal Mayor require the designated General Services Officer (GSO) to submit the complete RPCPPE compliant to the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I using the form prescribed therein and the same must be certified correct by the Inventory Committee and approved by the Local Chief Executive.
observations.managementAction: During the conduct of audit, the Municipality was able to submit to the Audit Team their RPCPPE, compliant to the requirements mentioned in the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to continue in exerting extra efforts to complete the titling process of the four parcels of land in the name of the Municipality of Plaridel to protect the government in the subject lots against third party claims.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor stated that they already seek legal advice regarding the titling considering their condition.
observations.reason: They are still retrieving documents relevant to the titling process.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: a. require the Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly to the provision that all payment should be made payable to the suppliers; b. instruct municipal officials and employees to stop resorting to procurement and disbursement through reimbursement basis; and c. ensure that the proper procurement purchases are also implemented by the Bids and Awards Committee.
observations.managementAction: Aside from the aforementioned observations, it was noted that some purchases either lack the details, manner, and schedule of its procurement or not included at all in the Annual Procurement Plan. The Bids and Awards and Committee implemented some procurement processes.
observations.reason: Some purchases were still paid through check to the official/employee concerned as reimbursement. Disbursement through reimbursement of officials/employees still existed in CY 2019. Also, some purchases either lack details, manner, schedule or were not included in the APP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the designated General Services Officer to immediate cause the registration of the said motor vehicles and to closely monitor its yearly registration in order to avoid payment of penalty for late registration and interest over its assets.
observations.managementAction: The designated GSO was able to locate a photocopy of the certificate of registration and its official receipt dated July 24, 2014 for the Mini Dump Truck. They are still locating for the necessary documents to facilitate the registration of the said vehicles.
observations.reason: They are still locating for the necessary documents relevant to the titling process. See Observation No. 6, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to submit to the Office of the Auditor the copies of Purchase Orders and their supporting documents within five working days from issuance, and inform the deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance in compliance with COA Circulars No. 2009-001 and 96-010, respectively for the Audit Team to conduct timely review of the documents.
observations.managementAction: The GSO was able to submit copies of POs to the Audit Team, compliant to the requirements mentioned in the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to revert the unutilized LDRRMF of CYs 2012 and 2013 back to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund and be made available for other social services after enactment by the local sanggunian.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to revert the unutilized LDRRMF of CYs 2012 and 2013 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund in CY 2019. This fund is now available for use in other social services through subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: instruct the MPDC to maximize the utilization of the 20 percent development fund so as to ensure that intended construction beneficiaries can avail of the maximum benefits; also instruct the MPDC in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer to observe proper planning and timing so that projects programmed for the year can be fully implemented within the planned year.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC and the Office of the Municipal Engineer closely observed the proper planning and timing of the projects that were implemented in CY 2019 to maximize the use of the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: In CY 2019, the Audit Team observed the non-implementation of solid waste management projects in various barangays totaling P1,500,000.00 under the 20 percent development fund due to delays in the preparation of the necessary documents before its bidding process. See Observation No. 7, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) secure a copy and enforce strictly the provisions stipulated in the loan agreement entered into by the delinquent borrowers with the MSWD and exhaust all means to recover from defaulting borrowers; and (b) if the circumstances so warrant, initiate the preparation of the request for authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off receivables which remained outstanding in the books to be supported with the documents as itemized in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management coordinate with the BIR for the filing of claim for tax credit or refund, if still acceptable, or if not, initiate the preparation of the request for authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off receivables which remained outstanding in the books to be supported with the documents as itemized in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  (a) instruct the General Services Officers to improve the process of inventory management by keeping track of the movement of stocks from receipt until issuance for accounting,  control and accountability purposes; initiate the preparation of stock cards for the purchased supplies; and conduct periodic inventory of supplies as prescribed by  regulations; (b) consider the provision of budget for a storage room so that the procurement of supplies in significant quantities can be undertaken to lessen the administrative cost in frequent procurement; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant to improve compliance on the recording of purchases under the proper inventory account and to maintain supplies ledger cards for delivered stocks.

We recommended that Management direct the GSO to (a) exert efforts to secure the transfer certificate of  title for the  lot purchased by the Municipality; (b) request the COA Auditor to determine  the  appraisal cost  of the  unserviceable property and effect the immediate disposal and  derecognition  from the  Accounting books  and  Property Records; and (c) maintain Property Cards and Real Property Cards for land for easy reconciliation of the account;

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Local Development Council (a) continue to invest in high-impact development projects in the utilization of its 20% Development Fund so that the best possible desired level of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes may be achieved/attained; and (b) ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion period through close supervision and monitoring to maximize the development benefits and the early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management continue to be compliant with the criteria on disaster readiness to prevent loss of lives and properties arising from natural disasters or calamities and aim to further equip its MDRRMC personnel with advanced training on disaster preparedness and rescue operations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive continue to be compliant with the rules and regulations in the utilization of the financial assistance under the LGSF/ADM Program to assure the attainment of its objective to alleviate the living condition of the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that Management (a) continue to exert best efforts to be fully compliant with the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 by strictly administering the segregation of wastes at source to minimize the cost being incurred in the waste disposal by the contractor and the Municipality’s garbage trucks; and (b) involve closely the remaining seven (7) barangays to adopt the strict implementation of the Anti-Littering campaign of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management (a) continue to comply with the GAD program on the formulation and submission of GAD plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report; and (b) establish and submit the details of accomplishment reports together with its supporting documents to validate the expenses charged to GAD fund.

We recommended that Management continue to be compliant with Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 on the withholding of the appropriate taxes from its payment of transactions and ensure remittance thereof within the prescribed period and continue to remit all other collections held in trust due to Other National Government Agencies and Government Owned Controlled Corporations within the prescribed period of ten (10) days after the end of each month.

We have recommended that management direct the collectors to remit intact all collections at the end of each business day to prevent any liability arising from possible losses resulting from unlawful use or application thereof, and for all losses attributable to negligence in keeping of the funds; and
Require the Liquidating Officer to monitor the collectors’ compliance of the required daily remittance of collections and to report any non-compliance to the Municipal Treasurer for proper action.

We have recommended that management stop granting special purpose cash advances to persons not appointed as special disbursing officer and non-permanent employees of the LGU.   Management may designate a special disbursing officer provided the designee is a permanent employee and properly bonded in accordance with the law.  Further, refrain from granting cash advances not duly supported with detailed list/estimate of expenses to avoid excessive cash advance.

We have recommended that Management discontinue the grant of travel allowance particularly for hotel/lodging including meals if these are already covered by the registration fee of the participant, otherwise all subsequent excessive claims shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management (a) direct the Municipal Planning and Development office and Municipal Engineer to monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the projects under 20% Development Fund so that timely necessary interventions may be made for the attainment of the objectives of the programs; (b) direct the other responsible officials concerned to address the issue that impede the full implementation of priority projects; (c) continue to invest in high-impact or development projects and include in the Annual Investment; and (d) make representation and coordinate with other national government agencies to avoid duplications of programmed projects identified in their Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that Management continue to being compliant with the criteria on disaster readiness to prevent loss of lives and properties arising from natural disasters or calamities, on the provision of funds for the local disaster risk reduction and management fund and in institutionalizing the disaster risk reduction and management plan pursuant to the requirements of RA 10121.

We recommended that Management (a) continue being compliant with the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000; (b) to fully impose compliance by the households to the ordinances and the provisions of the law and should strictly administer the segregation of wastes at source; and (c) involve closely the remaining seven (7) barangay officials to adopt the strict implementation of the Anti-Littering campaign of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management (a) require the MPDO to prepare the  Annual Investment Plan (AIP) which shall clearly specify the  targeted programs, projects or activities to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund; (b) direct the Municipal and Development Council to re-appropriate balances of the fund for the ensuing year to finance only those development programs, projects or activities identified under the existing regulations; (c) ensure  that the MPDO incorporate only eligible projects in accordance with the DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011–1 dated April 11, 2011; and (d) direct the Municipal Accountant to exercise diligence in the processing and charging of claims in accordance with rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management (a) provide adequate and realistic budget for its GAD Plan; (b)  require the Offices of the Municipal Agriculture and Municipal Social Welfare and Development to formulate guidelines on the eligibility of beneficiaries and the manner of distribution as basis for the assistance extended on agriculture and scholarship programs; (c) establish sex disaggregated data to institutionalize and improve GAD planning and budgeting;  and (d) enact a GAD Code and implement a comprehensive and sustainable GAD programs.

Justify disbursements from GAD Budget by indicating what gender issues and concerns it addresses supported by the GAD Plan.

Require the submission of the local government unit’s GAD Plan to the National Commission on the Role of Filipino Women (NCRFW) for endorsement.

Refrain payments from the GAD Budget for activities and programs not included in its plan.
